leagueBriefing: Championship Monday saw UConn continue its reign of terror in the Big East, while James Madison, Fairfield, and Jacksonville punched their tickets to the Big Dance with conference titles. Meanwhile, the Mountain West and West Coast tournaments set the stage for high-stakes finals after a day of semifinal drama.

games.headline: UConn captures 24th Big East title in dominant fashion over Villanova
games.brief: The top-ranked Huskies left no room for doubt at Mohegan Sun Arena, dismantling Villanova to secure their 24th Big East championship. From a crushing 15-3 opening run to a dominant interior performance, UConn looked every bit like the national title favorite, extending their perfect season behind a balanced attack that the Wildcats simply couldn't weather.
bullets: Azzi Fudd paced the Huskies with 19 points on efficient 8-of-13 shooting.
Sarah Strong dominated the glass and the paint with 18 points and eight rebounds in just 22 minutes.
UConn outscored Villanova 28-4 in the paint during a decisive first half.
The Huskies improved to 34-0 on the year, entering the NCAA Tournament undefeated.
games.context: UConn earns the Big East automatic bid and secures a likely No. 1 overall seed in the NCAA Tournament.

highlights.title: #1 UConn vs Villanova Highlights | Women's Basketball Championship | College Basketball 2026

games.headline: James Madison suffocates Troy to claim second Sun Belt crown in four years
games.brief: James Madison used a suffocating 13-0 start to take the air out of the Pensacola Bay Center and never looked back against the second-seeded Trojans. Troy's high-powered offense went cold under the Dukes' defensive pressure, missing their first 11 shots and failing to regain momentum even after a brief second-quarter rally.
bullets: Tournament MVP Peyton McDaniel led all scorers with 28 points and 10 rebounds.
Ashanti Barnes added a massive 19-point, 12-rebound double-double for the Dukes.
JMU held Troy to just 30% shooting from the field and 8% from beyond the arc.
The victory marks JMU's 12th consecutive win and their 14th NCAA Tournament appearance.
games.context: James Madison secures the Sun Belt's automatic NCAA Tournament berth.

games.headline: Fairfield cements MAAC dynasty with third consecutive conference title
games.brief: In a defensive grind in Atlantic City, Fairfield proved why they are the class of the MAAC, holding off top-seeded Quinnipiac to complete a championship three-peat. The Stags' defense was the story, forcing 19 turnovers and coming up with clutch stops in the final minute to deny the Bobcats a late comeback attempt.
bullets: Jillian Huerter earned Tournament MVP honors after hitting four critical 3-pointers for 16 points.
Cyanne Coe anchored the interior with a 15-point, 10-rebound double-double.
Fairfield has now reached the NCAA Tournament four times in the last five seasons.
Quinnipiac's Anna Foley led the Bobcats with 17 points and nine rebounds in the loss.
games.context: Fairfield claims the MAAC automatic bid and its seventh conference title in program history.

games.headline: Jacksonville edges Austin Peay in overtime thriller to clinch ASUN title
games.brief: Jacksonville survived a wild, back-and-forth overtime battle to claim its first ASUN championship in a decade. After seeing an 11-point lead evaporate in the fourth quarter, the Dolphins found their footing in the extra period, with Comari Mitchell's clutch layup providing the go-ahead points and a desperate Austin Peay three-pointer clanging off the rim at the buzzer.
bullets: Makiya Miller was named Tournament Most Outstanding Player after finishing with 49 points over three games.
Priscilla Williams recorded a huge double-double with 16 points and 13 rebounds before fouling out.
Austin Peay's Mya Williams led the Governors with 20 points in the losing effort.
This is Jacksonville's second-ever ASUN title and their first NCAA trip since 2016.
games.context: Jacksonville secures the ASUN automatic bid to the NCAA Tournament.

games.headline: Air Force stuns Boise State to keep Mountain West Cinderella run alive
games.brief: The Air Force Falcons are the talk of Las Vegas after pulling off yet another massive upset, this time toppling Boise State to reach the Mountain West final. After knocking off the top-seeded San Diego State earlier, the Falcons used a late 9-0 run to flip the game on its head and survived a furious Broncos rally to punch their ticket to the championship game.
bullets: Milahnie Perry scored a game-high 26 points and sealed the win with a clutch free throw.
Libby Hutton carried Boise State with a career-high 25 points and seven rebounds.
The Falcons outscored the Broncos 21-16 in a high-tension fourth quarter.
Air Force is making just its second-ever appearance in the Mountain West semifinals/finals.
games.context: The 9-seed Falcons advance to the Mountain West Championship as the lowest seed to ever do so.

games.headline: Colorado State shuts down UNLV to reach Mountain West final
games.brief: Colorado State spoiled the party for the home crowd at Thomas & Mack Center, pulling away from UNLV in the fourth quarter to secure a spot in the Mountain West title game. The Rams' defense was impenetrable in the final stretch, and back-to-back triples to open the fourth quarter gave them a cushion they would never surrender against the second-seeded Lady Rebels.
bullets: Brooke Carlson led the Rams' offense with 19 points and clutch scoring in the second half.
UNLV's Aaliyah Alexander scored a game-high 22 points in a valiant losing effort.
The Rams won the battle in the paint 28-10, proving too physical for the Rebels down low.
CSU shot a perfect 8-for-8 from the free-throw line to seal the victory late.
games.context: Colorado State advances to the Mountain West Championship to face Cinderella Air Force.

games.headline: Gonzaga blitzes Santa Clara to set up WCC title rematch
games.brief: Gonzaga looked every bit the powerhouse in Las Vegas, overwhelming Santa Clara from the perimeter and the paint to advance to the WCC title game. The Bulldogs shot a blistering 64% from three-point range and dominated the glass, turning a competitive early matchup into a runaway victory that sends a message to the rest of the conference.
bullets: Allie Turner led the Bulldogs with 17 points, while freshman sensation Lauren Whittaker added 16 and 9 rebounds.
Gonzaga out-rebounded the Broncos 50-32 and doubled them up on points in the paint (48-28).
Santa Clara was led by Ashley Hawkins, who put up 17 points in the loss.
The win sets up a championship showdown with Oregon State.
games.context: Gonzaga advances to the WCC Championship game for the eighth consecutive year.

games.headline: Oregon State holds off LMU rally to return to WCC final
games.brief: Oregon State survived a late scare from a gritty Loyola Marymount squad to punch their ticket to the WCC Championship. After building a comfortable lead through three quarters, the Beavers had to withstand a furious 24-point fourth-quarter explosion from the Lions, eventually relying on veteran poise to close out the win in the final minutes.
bullets: LMU's Amaya Lawson poured in 25 points to keep the Lions in the game until the final whistle.
The Beavers used a balanced attack, outscoring LMU 28-24 in a high-scoring final period.
Oregon State reached its 23rd win of the season with the victory.
The win earns the Beavers a rematch with Gonzaga for the WCC title.
games.context: Oregon State advances to the WCC Championship game seeking its first tournament title since joining the conference.

games.headline: Vermont survives double-overtime epic against UMBC to reach fourth straight final
games.brief: In a game that refused to end, top-seeded Vermont outlasted UMBC in a double-overtime thriller at Patrick Gym. UMBC's Jade Tillman put on a performance for the ages with 32 points, including a buzzer-beating layup to force overtime, but the Catamounts' depth and experience proved just enough to survive the Retrievers' upset bid and keep their championship hopes alive.
bullets: Jadyn Weltz led the Catamounts with 23 points and clutch perimeter shooting.
Nikola Priede reached the 1,000-point milestone while adding 22 points in the win.
UMBC's Jade Tillman set a career-high with 32 points and 10 rebounds.
Vermont clinched the victory with a 10-7 run in the second overtime period.
games.context: Vermont advances to the America East Championship for the fourth consecutive year.

games.headline: Maine edges Binghamton in semifinal battle to reach America East final
games.brief: Maine survived a fourth-quarter surge from Binghamton to keep their championship dreams alive in Orono. Despite a 24-point explosion from the Bearcats' Bella Pucci, the Black Bears relied on conference Player of the Year Adrianna Smith and a clutch free-throw performance from Sarah Talon to secure the win and return to the America East title game.
bullets: Adrianna Smith recorded her 57th career double-double with 26 points and 12 rebounds.
Sarah Talon scored 17 points and went 4-for-4 from the line in the final seconds to seal it.
Binghamton's Bella Pucci hit five three-pointers on her way to a team-high 24 points.
Maine forced 19 turnovers while committing only 8 giveaways in a disciplined performance.
games.context: Maine advances to the America East Championship seeking its 11th conference title.

games.headline: Freshman Mesuch's record-breaking night leads Navy past BU
games.brief: Zoe Mesuch put on a shooting clinic for the ages, breaking the Patriot League Tournament record with eight triples to lead top-seeded Navy past Boston University. The Terriers hung tough behind 33 points from Anete Adler, but Navy's explosive 20-2 run bridging the third and fourth quarters turned a one-point nail-biter into a comfortable home victory.
bullets: Freshman Zoe Mesuch scored a career-high 31 points and set a PL tourney record with 8 made 3-pointers.
Patriot Player of the Year Zanai Barnett-Gay added 16 points and 14 rebounds.
BU's Anete Adler tied a tournament record with 15 field goals on her way to 33 points.
The Mids used a 24-point third quarter to take control of the game.
games.context: Navy advances to the Patriot League semifinals to host defending champion Lehigh.

games.headline: Holy Cross throttles Colgate in dominant quarterfinal showing
games.brief: Holy Cross left no doubt in their Patriot League opener, suffocating Colgate from the opening tip to secure a lopsided quarterfinal win. Meg Cahalan ignited a 13-4 start and never let up, while the Crusaders' defense held the Raiders to a dismal 23% shooting from the floor in one of the most dominant defensive performances of the tournament.
bullets: Meg Cahalan led all scorers with 21 points in just 23 minutes of action.
Simone Foreman posted a double-double with 10 points and 13 rebounds.
Holy Cross held Colgate to just 36 points, including a five-point fourth quarter.
The Crusaders improved to 12-3 at home this season with the victory.
games.context: Holy Cross advances to the Patriot League semifinals to face Army West Point.

games.headline: Lovette's career night propels Lehigh past Loyola into semifinals
games.brief: Gracyn Lovette chose the perfect time for a career performance, leading Lehigh to a comfortable victory over Loyola Maryland. The Mountain Hawks jumped out to an early 10-point lead and maintained control throughout, answering every Greyhounds rally with timely shooting to set up a semifinal clash with top-seeded Navy.
bullets: Gracyn Lovette scored a career-high 19 points and added eight assists.
Belle Bramer chipped in 16 points and three steals for the Mountain Hawks.
Loyola's Lex Therien finished her career as the Patriot League's all-time rebounding leader (1,250).
Lehigh held Loyola to just 27% shooting in a decisive fourth quarter.
games.context: Lehigh advances to the Patriot League semifinals for the second consecutive year.

games.headline: Army pulls away late to fend off Lafayette's upset bid
games.brief: In a physical Patriot League quarterfinal, Army West Point used a late surge to overcome a stubborn Lafayette team. The Black Knights trailed by one late in the fourth quarter before unleashing a 14-5 run to close the game, anchored by a monster double-double from Kya Smith that proved to be the difference in the final minutes.
bullets: Kya Smith dominated with 21 points and a game-high 21 rebounds.
Army forced a decisive 14-5 run over the final four minutes of the game.
Lafayette was led by Teresa Kiewiet's 18 points and eight rebounds.
This win marks Army's fifth straight victory over the Leopards.
games.context: Army advances to the Patriot League semifinals to face No. 2 seed Holy Cross.

games.headline: Mercyhurst weathers Pelletier's 27-point night to advance
games.brief: Mercyhurst overcame a lights-out shooting performance from Stonehill's Ella Pelletier to secure a hard-fought win in the NEC quarterfinals. Stonehill led for much of the first half, but the Lakers' defense adjusted after the break, limiting the Skyhawks' supporting cast and pulling away late in the fourth quarter to clinch their spot in the semifinals.
bullets: Stonehill's Ella Pelletier scored a career-high 27 points, including four 3-pointers.
Mercyhurst outscored Stonehill 25-20 in a back-and-forth fourth quarter.
Freshman Julia Webster added 11 points and 8 assists for the Skyhawks.
The win moves Mercyhurst to .500 on the season (15-15).
games.context: Mercyhurst advances to the NEC semifinals in their first year of Division I eligibility.

games.headline: Postell's late dagger sends Le Moyne to NEC semifinals
games.brief: Le Moyne survived a defensive slugfest on Staten Island, relying on a late heroics from Sydney Postell to push past Wagner. In a game defined by scoring droughts and lead changes, Postell's 3-pointer with 45 seconds left finally broke the Seahawks' spirit, capping an 18-9 third-quarter surge that proved to be the difference.
bullets: Eli Clark led all scorers with 15 points, including two key triples.
Sydney Postell hit the game-winning three-pointer in the final minute.
The Dolphins reached the conference semifinals for the eighth time in nine years.
Le Moyne held Wagner to just 32% shooting from the field.
games.context: Le Moyne advances to the NEC semifinals in its second year in Division I.

games.headline: Bethune-Cookman holds off Texas Southern in SWAC opener
games.brief: Bethune-Cookman survived a late comeback attempt from Texas Southern to advance in the SWAC tournament. After building an eight-point halftime lead, the Wildcats had to weather a final Tigers surge that cut the deficit to just two points in the closing seconds before sealing the victory at the free-throw line.
bullets: Daimoni Dorsey and Mya Johnson each scored 12 points to lead the Wildcats.
Taliyah Longwood led all scorers with 14 points for Texas Southern.
The two teams combined for 57 free-throw attempts in a whistle-heavy contest.
B-CU forced 19 turnovers and recorded 11 steals to maintain their lead.
games.context: Bethune-Cookman advances to the second round of the SWAC Tournament.

games.headline: Northwestern State explodes in second half to down East Texas A&M
games.brief: Northwestern State looked like two different teams in Lake Charles, shaking off a sluggish first half to dominate the second. Behind a historic performance from SLC Player of the Year Vernell Atamah, the Lady Demons turned a halftime deficit into a comfortable double-digit victory, fueled by a 15-0 fourth-quarter run that left the Lions in the dust.
bullets: Vernell Atamah scored 14 of her 19 points in the fourth quarter and set a school 3-point record.
The Lady Demons dominated the glass, out-rebounding the Lions 53-26.
East Texas A&M led 32-23 at the half before being outscored 46-24 after the break.
NSU shot a near-perfect 14-of-15 from the free-throw line.
games.context: Northwestern State advances to the second round of the Southland Tournament.

games.headline: Green Bay crushes Purdue Fort Wayne to reach fourth straight title game
games.brief: Top-seeded Green Bay left no room for an upset in Indianapolis, overwhelming Purdue Fort Wayne with a dominant defensive display. A 16-0 first-half run effectively ended the contest early, and the Phoenix never looked back, controlling the paint and the glass to secure their fourth consecutive appearance in the Horizon League championship game.
bullets: Carley Duffney led the way with a season-high 20 points.
Meghan Schultz recorded a double-double with 13 points and 10 rebounds.
Purdue Fort Wayne was held to just 16.7% shooting from three-point range.
Green Bay dominated the boards, out-rebounding the Mastodons 47-27.
games.context: Green Bay advances to the Horizon League Championship to face Youngstown State.

games.headline: Youngstown State stuns Cleveland State with furious late rally
games.brief: Youngstown State is heading to its first-ever Horizon League championship game after a wild fourth-quarter comeback against Cleveland State. The Penguins trailed late but finished the game on a 6-0 run, fueled by a high-pressure defense and a clutch corner triple from Danielle Cameron that finally broke the Vikings' hold on the game.
bullets: Danielle Cameron led the Penguins with 16 points, 10 of which came in the fourth quarter.
YSU outscored Cleveland State 25-15 in the final period.
The Vikings' Izabella Zingaro put up a monster 19-point, 14-rebound performance in the loss.
Cleveland State missed all five of its three-point attempts in the fourth quarter.
games.context: Youngstown State reaches its first Horizon League Championship game in program history.

games.headline: Eastern Washington flips script on Northern Colorado to reach semis
games.brief: Eastern Washington overcame an 11-point halftime deficit to pull off a gritty upset over third-seeded Northern Colorado. The Eagles' defense was the story in the fourth quarter, holding the Bears to just seven points and 27% shooting, as they snapped a four-game losing streak against UNC at the most critical time possible.
bullets: Five Eagles scored in double figures, led by Ella Gallatin and Kourtney Grossman with 11 each.
Eastern Washington outscored Northern Colorado 35-22 in the second half.
Jaecy Eggers posted her 6th double-double of the season with 10 points and 11 rebounds.
The Eagles improved to 4-0 this season when holding opponents under 30% shooting.
games.context: Eastern Washington advances to the Big Sky semifinals.

games.headline: Idaho State stifles Sacramento State in first-round rout
games.brief: Idaho State rode a dominant defensive performance and a record-breaking night from freshman Jackson Rasmussen to brush past Sacramento State in Boise. The Vandals held the Hornets to a mere 13 points in the first half, allowing them to cruise through the second period and secure back-to-back years with a conference tournament win for the first time in a decade.
bullets: Jackson Rasmussen became the all-time leading freshman scorer in school history with 19 points.
Idaho State's defense tied the lowest scoring total by an opponent in a Big Sky tourney game in a decade (45 at one point).
Kolton Mitchell added 17 points and eight rebounds in his return from injury.
Sacramento State was held to just 25% shooting from the floor.
games.context: Idaho State advances to the Big Sky semifinals to face Montana State.
